title: Micranthes foliolosa
intro: Micranthes foliolosa, also known as the Alpine Bitterflower, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the Saxifragaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has shown that it may offer health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The bioactive constituents of the Alpine Bitterflower include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Micranthes foliolosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Micranthes foliolosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Saxifragaceae. Within this family, it is part of the genus Micranthes. The Saxifragaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as saxifrages. These plants are often found in moist, shaded areas and are native to the northern hemisphere. Micranthes foliolosa is also known as the small-leaf saxifrage.
uses: Micranthes foliolosa, also known as the mountain avens, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and roots contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may be used to treat wounds and skin irritations. Additionally, mountain avens has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using mountain avens for medicinal purposes. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedies.

constituents: Micranthes foliolosa, also known as the mountain avens, contains active constituents such as flavonoids and triterpene saponins.
